title: AI Threat Model Report: Demo Internal RAG Assistant

system_overview.name: Demo Internal RAG Assistant
system_overview.description: A fake internal assistant that answers employee questions using retrieved company policy documents. This sample is for defensive AI security threat modeling practice only.

data_flows: User submits a question to the assistant.
The application sends the question to a retrieval system.
The retrieval system returns relevant policy document chunks.
The application combines user input and retrieved context into a model prompt.
The application returns the answer to the user.
The system stores basic logs for review and troubleshooting.
assumptions: The system uses fake sample data for this project.
The assistant may retrieve internal documents.
Retrieved documents may contain untrusted or outdated content.
The model may produce inaccurate or unsupported answers.
The system does not perform autonomous external actions in this sample.

findings.description: The system may expose sensitive information through prompts, retrieved context, logs, tool results, or model responses.
findings.reason: The system handles sensitive data that could be exposed if controls are weak.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Minimize sensitive data use, redact unnecessary fields, enforce access controls, and review logs for sensitive content.

findings.description: Private documents may be retrieved or summarized for users who should not have access to them.
findings.reason: The system handles private documents that require strong authorization controls.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Enforce document-level access checks before retrieval and before response generation.

findings.description: User-controlled input may attempt to override system instructions or manipulate the model into producing unsafe or unauthorized output.
findings.reason: The system accepts natural language input from users.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Treat all user input as untrusted. Use instruction hierarchy, input validation, output filtering, and clear separation between user content and system instructions.

findings.description: Untrusted or manipulated documents in a retrieval system may influence the model to generate incorrect, unsafe, or misleading answers.
findings.reason: RAG systems depend on retrieved documents that may not all be equally trustworthy.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Use document source validation, ingestion review, document versioning, and trusted-source labeling.

findings.description: Retrieved context may contain outdated, inaccurate, malicious, or irrelevant content that the model treats as reliable.
findings.reason: Retrieved context is often mixed directly into model prompts.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Label retrieved content as untrusted context, validate document sources, and require citations or confidence notes where appropriate.

findings.description: The system may perform or recommend sensitive actions without human review or approval.
findings.reason: Human approval is important when AI output can affect users, records, or decisions.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Require human review for sensitive, external, financial, legal, account, or irreversible actions.

findings.description: Users may access AI features, documents, logs, or outputs that should be restricted.
findings.reason: Authentication alone does not guarantee proper authorization.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Use role-based access control, document-level authorization, and regular permission reviews.

findings.description: Prompts, responses, retrieved context, or tool results may be logged with sensitive data included.
findings.reason: AI logs often contain user input and model output that may include sensitive content.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Redact sensitive data, limit log retention, restrict log access, and avoid logging unnecessary prompt or document content.

findings.description: The model may generate incorrect or fabricated information that users mistakenly trust.
findings.reason: LLMs can generate confident but incorrect answers.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Use uncertainty language, verification steps, citations, and human review for important decisions.

findings.description: Users may rely too heavily on model output without understanding its limitations or uncertainty.
findings.reason: AI systems can produce confident answers even when uncertain.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Add clear disclaimers, confidence indicators, review workflows, and user training about model limitations.

findings.description: Users may attempt to bypass system restrictions or safety instructions through adversarial prompting.
findings.reason: Public or user-facing LLM systems are likely to receive adversarial prompts.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Use layered safety controls, refusal handling, prompt hardening, and monitoring for repeated abuse patterns.

findings.description: The system may retain prompts, responses, files, or retrieved context longer than needed.
findings.reason: Stored AI interaction data can become a privacy and compliance risk.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Define retention periods, delete unnecessary data, and document retention rules.

findings.description: The system may lack monitoring for abuse, failures, unsafe output, or unexpected model behavior.
findings.reason: AI systems need monitoring because failures may be subtle or repeated over time.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Track high-risk prompts, blocked responses, tool calls, access failures, and user reports.

findings.description: The system may not preserve enough evidence to understand what happened during a security or safety incident.
findings.reason: Without audit trails, teams may not be able to investigate incidents effectively.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Record important actions, approvals, tool calls, policy decisions, and access events.

findings.description: The model may reveal hidden system instructions, internal policies, or configuration details in its responses.
findings.reason: LLM applications often rely on hidden instructions that users may try to extract.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Avoid placing secrets or sensitive operational details in system prompts. Design prompts assuming they may be exposed.

findings.description: The model may produce content that appears to reveal memorized or sensitive training data.
findings.reason: Some model outputs may reflect sensitive or memorized training examples.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Use models and providers with clear data handling policies, avoid training on sensitive data unless approved, and test for leakage patterns.

findings.description: The system may generate citations that do not support the answer or refer to sources that were not actually used.
findings.reason: Citation-generating systems can create misleading confidence if sources are weak.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Verify citations against retrieved source text and clearly indicate when an answer is unsupported.

findings.description: The system may return model output without checking for format errors, unsafe content, unsupported claims, or policy violations.
findings.reason: Model output should not automatically be treated as safe or correct.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Validate outputs for required format, policy compliance, citations, and sensitive content.

findings.description: The system may produce responses that users interpret as authoritative, which could support manipulation, confusion, or unsafe decisions.
findings.reason: AI responses can appear confident and persuasive even when incomplete or wrong.
findings.recommended_mitigation: Use careful wording, user education, escalation paths, and human review for high-impact guidance.

prioritized_action_plan.action: Minimize sensitive data use, redact unnecessary fields, enforce access controls, and review logs for sensitive content.
Enforce document-level access checks before retrieval and before response generation.
Treat all user input as untrusted. Use instruction hierarchy, input validation, output filtering, and clear separation between user content and system instructions.
Use document source validation, ingestion review, document versioning, and trusted-source labeling.
Label retrieved content as untrusted context, validate document sources, and require citations or confidence notes where appropriate.
Require human review for sensitive, external, financial, legal, account, or irreversible actions.
Use role-based access control, document-level authorization, and regular permission reviews.
Redact sensitive data, limit log retention, restrict log access, and avoid logging unnecessary prompt or document content.
Use uncertainty language, verification steps, citations, and human review for important decisions.
Add clear disclaimers, confidence indicators, review workflows, and user training about model limitations.
Use layered safety controls, refusal handling, prompt hardening, and monitoring for repeated abuse patterns.
Define retention periods, delete unnecessary data, and document retention rules.
Track high-risk prompts, blocked responses, tool calls, access failures, and user reports.
Record important actions, approvals, tool calls, policy decisions, and access events.
Avoid placing secrets or sensitive operational details in system prompts. Design prompts assuming they may be exposed.
Use models and providers with clear data handling policies, avoid training on sensitive data unless approved, and test for leakage patterns.
Verify citations against retrieved source text and clearly indicate when an answer is unsupported.
Validate outputs for required format, policy compliance, citations, and sensitive content.
Use careful wording, user education, escalation paths, and human review for high-impact guidance.

disclaimer: This report is generated by a simple local rules-based tool for defensive education and portfolio demonstration. It is not a full professional security assessment, legal review, compliance audit, or production architecture review.
